title: Pilosella banaticola
taxonomy: Pilosella banaticola is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants commonly known as wood sorrels. The Pilosella genus is further divided into several subgenera, with Pilosella banaticola being classified under the subgenus Caespitella. Within the subgenus Caespitella, Pilosella banaticola is considered a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and distribution. Pilosella banaticola is native to Romania and is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and fragmentation.

intro: Pilosella banaticola, also known as Banatic dandelion, is a rare and lesser-known species within the genus Pilosella, a group of flowering plants in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella banaticola include sesquiterpene lactones, such as pilosulin, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilosella banaticola is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential health-promoting and therapeutic effects.
